props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison

beats.narration_text: Yassou. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of quantum platform n_gates comparison. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of quantum platform n_gates comparison

beats.narration_text: Here is why Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. N_gates = T₂ / t_gate — figure of merit for fault-tolerant quantum computing. Fault-tolerance threshold: N_gates > 10⁴.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: t_gate scale:. Verify: N_gates = T₂ / t_gate — figure of merit for fault-tolerant quantum computing. Fault-tolerance threshold: N_gates > 10⁴.. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: t_gate scale:. Verify: N_gates = T₂

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.

Now drag t_gate scale: from -1 to 1. Watch the physics respond in real time.

beats.narration_text: N_gates = T₂ / t_gate — figure of merit for fault-tolerant quantum computing. Fault-tolerance threshold: N_gates > 10⁴.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of quantum platform n_gates comparison. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Quantum Platform N_gates Comparison simulation and add a second slider that controls t_gate scale:. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
